title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: Two changes requested: (1) In `docs/reference/container-images.md:309`, update the stale description of the constraints file path from "current directory" to `releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt` to match the actual script behavior. (2) In `images/python-base/Dockerfile:17`, prepend `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to the `apt-get` `RUN` step to prevent potential interactive `tzdata` prompts during Docker builds.

code_comments.body:
**Stale description of script's file location**

This sentence says the script reads `upper-constraints.txt` "from the current directory", which was accurate for the original implementation but is no longer correct. The script now resolves `CONSTRAINTS="releases/${RELEASE}/upper-constraints.txt"` (line 16 of the script), so the file read is at `releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt` relative to the current working directory â€” not directly at `./upper-constraints.txt`.

```suggestion
The script reads `releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt` relative to the current working
directory (must be invoked from the repository root) and modifies it in-place. It uses GNU
`sed -i` for modifications (default on Ubuntu/CI runners â€” BSD `sed` is not supported).
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: The description at docs/reference/container-images.md:307-309 was updated from stating the script reads `upper-constraints.txt` from the current directory to correctly stating it reads `releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt` relative to the current working directory, with a note that it must be invoked from the repository root. The suggested text was applied verbatim.

code_comments.body:
**`tzdata` install may prompt without `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`**

`tzdata` is known to present interactive prompts for geographic area and timezone when installed via `apt-get` if `DEBIAN_FRONTEND` is not set. Docker builds have no TTY, so `tzdata` usually defaults to UTC without hanging, but the behaviour is environment-dependent and has caused silent build failures on some CI runners. Setting `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` for the duration of this `RUN` step makes the behaviour explicit and portable.

```suggestion
RUN DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    netbase \
    python3 \
    sudo \
    tzdata \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: The `RUN` instruction at images/python-base/Dockerfile:11 was updated to prepend `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` before `apt-get update`, making the tzdata installation explicitly non-interactive. The suggested code was applied verbatim.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed exactly as suggested. Comment [0] about the stale file location description in container-images.md was fixed by updating lines 307-309 to reference `releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt` with the repository root invocation requirement. Comment [1] about the tzdata interactive prompt risk in the Dockerfile was fixed by prepending `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to the `apt-get` command on line 11.
